subject: HSC Mathematics Extension 2
description: Index of marking guidelines by year. Each entry references the full marking guideline data embedded in the question bank. This file provides a quick lookup for the marking-guideline-feedback skill.

partialCreditPolicy.description: OpenTutor partial credit assessment policy, aligned with NESA principles

rules.condition: Correct first step but incorrect subsequent steps
Correct answer without required working shown
Follow-through error: error from part (a) carried correctly into part (b)
'Show that' question: answer is given, so full working is mandatory
rules.marksAwarded: 0 if working absent, even if conclusion is stated

rules.condition: Proof question: working both sides simultaneously
rules.marksAwarded: 0 — must work from one side only

ext2SpecificPolicies.description: Additional marking policies specific to Extension 2 question types

policies.policy: Full stepwise working required. Conclusion must follow logically from working. No marks for stating the result alone.
Base case, explicit inductive assumption, and full inductive step are all required. A conclusion statement ('by induction, true for all n ≥ ...') is mandatory for full marks.
The initial assumption must be clearly stated. The contradiction must be made explicit. Conclusion must follow from the contradiction.
The contrapositive statement must be clearly written before the proof begins. The logical link back to the original statement must be stated.
Argument must lie in the principal range (-π, π]. Arguments outside this range receive 0 for the argument component.
Locus must be described both algebraically (equation) and geometrically (e.g., 'perpendicular bisector', 'circle', 'ray'). Half-lines must state the excluded endpoint.
All roots must be found. For nth roots, exactly n distinct values are required. Geometric description of root arrangement must be stated where asked.
Choice of u and dv must be stated explicitly. The formula ∫u dv = uv − ∫v du must be applied correctly with all terms shown.
The substitution, the transformed integrand, and the change of limits (for definite integrals) must all be shown. Back-substitution required for indefinite integrals.
The derivation must use integration by parts with all intermediate steps shown. The boundary term evaluation must be explicit. Application of the formula must show each step of the recurrence.
Vector and Cartesian forms must be clearly distinguished. When finding intersection, all three component equations must be written and the third must be verified for consistency.

policies.questionType: Vectors — cross product and scalar triple product
policies.policy: Determinant expansion must be shown in full, including the alternating signs. Volume formulas using the scalar triple product must include the correct fraction (1/6 for tetrahedron, 1/2 for parallelepiped base area).

generalPrinciples: Marks are awarded for correct mathematical reasoning and working, not just the final answer
Where a question says 'show that' or 'prove', full working must be shown — the answer is given
Correct answer without working may receive zero marks for multi-mark questions
If a student makes an error early but carries through consistently, follow-through marks may be awarded
Diagrams must be clearly labelled to receive associated marks
Rounding: round only at the final step unless otherwise instructed
Units must be included where applicable
Arbitrary constants in integration results must be shown (write + C)
For induction proofs, base case, inductive assumption and inductive step must all be present and clearly labelled

sectionI.marking: No working required. 1 mark for correct answer, 0 otherwise.

sectionII.description: Extended response questions requiring full working
sectionII.marking: Criteria-based marking. See individual question entries in ext2-questions.json for full criteria.
generalNotes: Show all necessary working
Marks may be awarded for correct intermediate steps even if the final answer is wrong
State conclusions in the context of the problem
For complex number locus questions, both algebraic and geometric descriptions are required
For proof questions, begin from one side only and work to the other
For induction proofs, all three components (base case, assumption, step) must be explicitly labelled

2023-ME2-Q11a.keySkill: complex division — multiply by conjugate to reach a + bi form

2023-ME2-Q11b.keySkill: modulus-argument form — correct quadrant and principal argument

2023-ME2-Q12.keySkill: integration by parts — ∫x sin x dx with correct u and dv selection

2023-ME2-Q13.keySkill: complex locus — perpendicular bisector from |z − a| = |z − b|

2023-ME2-Q14.keySkill: 3D vector lines — intersection via simultaneous component equations

2023-ME2-Q15.keySkill: trig integration — half-angle identity and arcsin substitution

2023-ME2-Q16.keySkill: proof by induction — sum of squares formula

2023-ME2-Q17.keySkill: partial fractions — decomposition and logarithmic integration

2023-ME2-Q18.keySkill: roots of unity — de Moivre's theorem and geometric arrangement

2023-ME2-Q19.keySkill: equation of a plane — normal via cross product, Cartesian form

2023-ME2-Q20.keySkill: reduction formula — derivation by parts and repeated application

2023-ME2-Q21.keySkill: de Moivre's theorem — cos(4θ) expansion and polynomial roots

2023-ME2-Q22.keySkill: proof by contrapositive and contradiction — irrationality of √2

2022-ME2-Q11a.keySkill: complex number arithmetic — powers and simplification using i² = −1

2022-ME2-Q11b.keySkill: complex conjugate — division and Cartesian form

2022-ME2-Q12.keySkill: complex locus — circle from |z − a| = r

2022-ME2-Q13.keySkill: integration by parts — tabular or repeated application

2022-ME2-Q14.keySkill: 3D vectors — dot product, angle and perpendicularity

2022-ME2-Q15.keySkill: partial fractions — repeated or irreducible quadratic factor

2022-ME2-Q16.keySkill: proof by induction — divisibility or inequality

2022-ME2-Q17.keySkill: trigonometric substitution — definite integral with limit change

2022-ME2-Q18.keySkill: de Moivre's theorem — multiple angle formula derivation

2022-ME2-Q19.keySkill: vector equation of a plane — normal vector and point substitution

2022-ME2-Q20.keySkill: complex roots of unity — sum/product properties and exact trig values

2022-ME2-Q21.keySkill: reduction formula — derivation and iterative evaluation

2022-ME2-Q22.keySkill: proof by contradiction — irrational or number-theoretic result

2021-ME2-Q11a.keySkill: complex numbers — argument and modulus of a product

2021-ME2-Q12.keySkill: integration by parts — ∫x eˣ dx or similar

2021-ME2-Q13.keySkill: complex locus — half-line from arg(z − a) = θ

2021-ME2-Q14.keySkill: 3D vector lines — parametric form and direction vector

2021-ME2-Q15.keySkill: partial fractions and integration — linear factors

2021-ME2-Q16.keySkill: proof by induction — geometric series or power inequality

2021-ME2-Q18.keySkill: de Moivre's theorem — sin(nθ) or cos(nθ) expansion

2021-ME2-Q19.keySkill: planes in 3D — distance from point to plane

2021-ME2-Q20.keySkill: roots of unity — factorisation and exact trigonometric values

2021-ME2-Q21.keySkill: reduction formula — sinⁿx or cosⁿx with boundary evaluation

2021-ME2-Q22.keySkill: proof by induction — inequality with inductive step requiring justification

2020-ME2-Q11a.keySkill: complex number powers — expanding and collecting real and imaginary parts

2020-ME2-Q11b.keySkill: 3D dot product — angle between vectors including perpendicularity

2020-ME2-Q12.keySkill: trigonometric substitution — x = a sinθ with definite integral limit change

2020-ME2-Q18.keySkill: complex locus — ray from arg condition combined with circle condition

2020-ME2-Q19.keySkill: integral symmetry property — substitution u = π − x and evaluation

2020-ME2-Q20.keySkill: primitive roots of unity — sum/product identities and exact cos(2π/5)

2020-ME2-Q21.keySkill: scalar triple product — volume of tetrahedron via cross and dot product

2020-ME2-Q22.keySkill: proof by induction — inequality 3ⁿ > n² and extension to n³
